title: Keep files and sharing permissions in good order
staff_situations: How do I keep files and sharing permissions in good order?
underlying_needs: Knowing who can see, edit and share a file, and checking this is set correctly before it's shared
Editable access to another learner's personal or 1:1 records must never happen — this is a practice rule, stated plainly, not a judgement call to make case by case
Reviewing and removing access when it's no longer needed
digital_practices: keeping 1:1 and personal records separate from shared group spaces

accessibility: Correctly scoped permissions mean a learner using assistive technology only encounters files intended for them, rather than a cluttered or confusing space
inclusive_practice: Protects every learner's personal and 1:1 records equally — this is not optional or dependent on who is asking
Clear permission structure prevents a learner from accidentally being given access to another learner's information
trauma_informed: Avoids the distress of a learner discovering their personal or 1:1 records were visible to someone they shouldn't have been

framework_alignment: Digital Skills Framework: file and permission management
